resources.string: Are you sure you want to delete this server? (This only deletes the server from the app, it does not delete the server files)
Path to the server installation folder, changing this will not move the existing installation.
Enables Ark Ascended Server API, which allows the use of plugins. These are not the same as mods. Requires reinstall.
The password users must enter in order to access the server. If this is set, only people who know the password can access your server.
The password users must enter to execute admin/cheat commands on the server. If this is not set, anyone can use cheats.
The port users will use to connect to your server. Default is 7777. Change this and Query Port if you wish to host multiple servers on one machine.
Peer port (always Game port +1), used for connecting to the server from the client.
The port Steam uses to communicate with your server. Default is 27015. Change this and Server Port if you wish to host multiple servers on one machine.
Enable RCON (Remote Console) for this server. This allows you to execute admin/cheat commands on the server.
The port that RCON will use on your server.
The map your server will load. Make sure to use the correct map name, otherwise your server might not start.
A comma-separated list of mod ids, in the order in which they should be applied.
Set how often the server will automatically save the game. Setting it to a very low value may impact performance, setting this to 0 will cause constant saving.
This message will be shown to players as a welcome message when they join the server.
Set how long the Message of the Day will be shown to players.
If enabled, this will allow downloading external survivors, items, and dinos into your server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading survivors from the server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading items from the server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading dinosaurs from the server.
If enabled, this allows non-native creatures tribute downloads on Aberration.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading survivors.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading items.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading dinosaurs.
Prevents ARK Data usage between single player and servers that do not have a cluster ID. Even with a cluster ID set, keep this enabled to fully disable code paths that allow it.
Limits number of dinosaurs that can be uploaded in a tribute. Default is 20. Range is 20-100.
Limits number of items that can be uploaded in a tribute. Default is 50. Range is 50-100.

Enable to write tribute character expiration in minutes. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.

Set, in minutes, the expiration timer for uploaded dinosaurs in ARK Data. If set to 0 or lower, the server will revert to default. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.

Set, in minutes, the expiration timer for uploaded items in ARK Data. If set to 0 or lower, the server will revert to default. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.
Sets the maximum number of players which can join this server.
Determines how many lines of game logs are send over the RCON.
If enabled, idle players will be kicked from the server after a specified amount of time.
The amount of time (in seconds) after which idle players will be kicked from the server. This option is only relevant if idle player kick is enabled. (3600 seconds is one hour)
Ban list URL, sets the global ban list.
Enables the use of the dynamic config
Direct link to the live tuning file.
Select the root folder of the server installation:
The root folder of the server installation. You can identify the installation path by looking for the 'ShooterGame' and 'Engine' folder in the server installation directory.
The selected path is not a valid server installation.
SteamCMD path not configured in settings.
Cluster ID is automatically set based on the selected cluster. (This field is used by ark to group servers together)
The location where cluster data like player and item uploads are stored (This field is used by ark to group servers together)
Override the server locale. This does not affect the client locale.
Disable BattlEye on the server. This will also disable BattlEye on the client.
Disables the Custom Cosmetic system (allowing players to use and display special mods that should only be skins and will be downloaded automatically by the connected clients).
Launch with this command to destroy all wild creatures on server start-up. Note: this will only destroy wild creatures that are not currently tamed.

Prevents wild creatures from being spawned. Enabling this option will make the following options unusable: -NoDinosExceptForcedSpawn, -NoDinosExceptStreamingSpawn, -NoDinosExceptManualSpawn and -NoDinosExceptWaterSpawn.
Chance for Easter colors when creatures spawn.
Specifies the scaling factor for creature taming speed. Higher values make taming faster.

Specifies the scaling factor for yields from all harvesting activities (chopping down trees, picking berries, carving carcasses, mining rocks, etc.). Higher values increase the amount of materials harvested with each strike.

Specifies the scaling factor for the "health" of items that can be harvested (trees, rocks, carcasses, etc.). Higher values increase the amount of damage (i.e., "number of strikes") such objects can withstand before being destroyed, which results in higher overall harvest yields.
Specifies the scaling factor for the re-spawn rate for resource nodes (trees, rocks, bushes, etc.). Lower values cause nodes to re-spawn more frequently.

Controls how resources regrow closer or farther away from players. Values higher than 1.0 increase the distance around players where resources are not allowed to grow back. Values between 0 and 1.0 will reduce it.

Controls how resources regrow closer or farther away from structures. Values higher than 1.0 increase the distance around structures where resources are not allowed to grow back. Values between 0 and 1.0 will reduce it.
Specifies the map base temperature scaling factor: lower value makes the environment colder, higher value makes the environment hotter.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK, controlling how often day changes to night and night changes to day. The default value 1 provides the same cycle speed as the single player experience (and the official public servers). Values lower than 1 slow down the cycle; higher values accelerate it. Base time when value is 1 appears to be 1-minute real time equals approx. 28-minutes game time. Thus, for an approximate 24-hour day/night cycle in game, use .035 for the value.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK during the day. This value determines the length of each day, relative to the length of each night (as specified by NightTimeSpeedScale). Lowering this value increases the length of each day.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK during the night. This value determines the length of each night, relative to the length of each day (as specified by DayTimeSpeedScale). Lowering this value increases the length of each night.
Scales the spoiling time of perishables globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the decomposition time of dropped items, loot bags etc. globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the decomposition time of corpses, (player and creature), globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the speed of crop decay in plots. A higher value decrease (by percentage) speed of crop decay in plots.
Scales the speed of crop growth in plots. A higher value increases (by percentage) speed of crop growth.
Scales the time between eggs are spawning / being laid. Higher number increases it (by percentage).
Scales how frequently survivors can poop. Higher value decreases it (by percentage).
Scales the hair growth. Higher values increase speed of growth.
Scales the amount of XP earned for generic XP (automatic over time).
Overrides items crafting resource requirements. See Item related section for more details.
Scales the amount of XP earned for harvesting.
Scale the amount of XP earned for a kill.
Scale the amount of XP earned for SpecialEvent.

If enabled, limit the damage caused by a tame to a resource on harvesting based on resource remaining health. Note: enabling this setting may result in sensible resource harvesting reduction using high damage tools or creatures.

If enabled, clamps all spoiling times to the items' maximum spoiling times. Useful if any infinite-spoiling exploits were used on the server and you wish to clean them up. Could potentially cause issues with mods that alter spoiling time, hence it is an option.
Automatically restart the server if it crashes, this is done within 5 seconds of the server crashing.
